name: 2026-05-19 Economic & Utility Reform Fuel Hike 98 Percent Imports TH
description:
Sino ba talaga ang nagbabayad?

May 19, 6 AM. Diesel up P2.82 per liter. Gasoline up P1.21. Kerosene down P2.21. Fourth straight week of gasoline hikes.

The Philippines imports 98% of its oil. A country that imports 98% of its energy does not negotiate prices.

It is told.

custom_fields.value:
Sino ba talaga ang nagbabayad?

May 19, 6 AM. Diesel up P2.82 per liter. Gasoline up P1.21. Kerosene down P2.21. Fourth straight week of gasoline hikes.

The Philippines imports 98% of its oil. A country that imports 98% of its energy does not negotiate prices.

It is told.

Sino ba talaga ang nagbabayad?

May 19, 6 AM. Diesel up P2.82 per liter. Gasoline up P1.21. Kerosene down P2.21. Fourth straight week of gasoline hikes.

The Philippines imports 98% of its oil. A country that imports 98% of its energy does not negotiate prices.

It is told.

Oil companies (Petron, Shell, Seaoil, Unioil) implement adjustments effective 6 AM PHT, May 19, 2026: diesel +P2.82/L, gasoline +P1.21/L, kerosene -P2.21/L. Rappler explicitly: fourth straight week of gasoline hikes. Backdrop: PH imports ~98% of its oil (Lowy Institute, GMA News explainer). Sources: Rappler, GMA News Online, Inquirer, Philippine News Agency.
